title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Prompt Lens: Change Scope
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt change scope increment in a coding-agent implementation brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Change Scope evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-change-scope-6dfh3b" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Change Scope into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-change-scope-6dfh3b
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Change Scope failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Change Scope, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Change Scope model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Change Scope applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
